planner_decisions.reason: Full JcaCipherService source available — IV generation method body retrieved.
Full Argon2Hash source available — all default parameter constants retrieved.
BlowfishCipherService source read. No production caller found in grep hits — class exists but caller search confirms no active production usage discovered.
PaddingScheme enum full body retrieved — all 10 values confirmed.
JdbcRealm.doGetAuthenticationInfo() full body retrieved — credential mechanism confirmed.
SaltAwareJdbcRealm.doGetAuthenticationInfo() full body retrieved — salt mechanism and security issue confirmed.

new_evidence_records.explanation: generateInitializationVector() creates a byte array of initializationVectorSize/8 bytes and fills it via SecureRandom.nextBytes(). encrypt() calls this method when generateInitializationVectors=true (default). IV is cryptographically random — generated by SecureRandom, not static or derived.

IV prepend-to-ciphertext behavior confirmed from method body: IV bytes copied to output[0..iv.length], then encrypted bytes appended. Output = IV || ciphertext. Decrypt path reads IV prefix before decrypting.

DEFAULT_KEY_SIZE=128. initializationVectorSize defaults to DEFAULT_KEY_SIZE (128 bits). generateInitializationVectors=true by default. SecureRandom algorithm is SHA1PRNG with fallback to platform default.

All default parameters confirmed: iterations=1, memory=65536 KiB (64 MiB), parallelism=4, output=256 bits, salt=128 bits. Taken from draft-irtf-cfrg-argon2-13, section 4.2 (memory-constrained environment). OWASP minimum for argon2id is m=19456 (19 MiB), t=2, p=1 — Shiro's 64 MiB, t=1 satisfies memory requirement but iteration count t=1 is at OWASP minimum boundary.

Full generate() call chain confirmed. Default entry point generate(char[]) uses DEFAULT_ITERATIONS=1. Full parameter path uses DEFAULT_MEMORY_KIB=65536, DEFAULT_PARALLELISM=4, DEFAULT_OUTPUT_LENGTH_BITS=256. Argon2BytesGenerator.generateBytes() is the BouncyCastle call site.

BlowfishCipherService confirmed: ALGORITHM_NAME='Blowfish', BLOCK_SIZE=64 bits (8 bytes). Default mode from parent class (DefaultBlockCipherService) is CBC with PKCS5 padding. 64-bit block size is the core weakness — birthday attack probability reaches 50% after ~2^32 blocks (~32 GB). No production caller found in grep sweep — class exists in public API but is not instantiated in non-test production code.

new_evidence_records.extracted_content:
public enum PaddingScheme {
    NONE("NoPadding"),
    ISO10126("ISO10126Padding"),
    OAEP("OAEPPadding"),
    OAEPWithMd5AndMgf1("OAEPWithMD5AndMGF1Padding"),
    OAEPWithSha1AndMgf1("OAEPWithSHA-1AndMGF1Padding"),
    OAEPWithSha256AndMgf1("OAEPWithSHA-256AndMGF1Padding"),
    OAEPWithSha384AndMgf1("OAEPWithSHA-384AndMGF1Padding"),
    OAEPWithSha512AndMgf1("OAEPWithSHA-512AndMGF1Padding"),
    PKCS1("PKCS1Padding"),
    PKCS5("PKCS5Padding"),
    SSL3("SSL3Padding")
}
new_evidence_records.explanation: Full PaddingScheme enum confirmed: 11 values. Notable: PKCS1Padding present — insecure for RSA (PKCS#1 v1.5 is vulnerable to Bleichenbacher/padding oracle attacks). OAEPWithMd5AndMgf1 present — MD5 in OAEP context is a legacy concern. PKCS5 and OAEP variants (SHA-256/384/512) are acceptable. NoPadding present — can be misused. No padding value is flagged as deprecated in source.

SaltStyle.NO_SALT is the DEFAULT. This means by default JdbcRealm retrieves the raw password column and passes it directly to the CredentialsMatcher WITHOUT salt. If the CredentialsMatcher is HashedCredentialsMatcher the stored value is a hash; if it is SimpleCredentialsMatcher the stored value is compared as plaintext. Default configuration is unsalted — security risk if deployer does not configure HashedCredentialsMatcher.

Full doGetAuthenticationInfo() body confirmed. Default saltStyle=NO_SALT returns raw password string from DB. EXTERNAL case calls getSaltForUser() which returns the username as salt — insecure default (predictable salt). Actual hash algorithm is NOT set in JdbcRealm — it is delegated to the CredentialsMatcher configured externally. No hash operation occurs in JdbcRealm itself.

SaltAwareJdbcRealm confirmed as sample code (samples/spring-mvc). Salt is set to ByteSource.Util.bytes(username) — the username string used as salt. Source code comment explicitly warns: 'This (very bad) example uses the username as the salt... DON'T DO THIS IN A REAL APP!' Predictable/weak salt: attacker who knows username can precompute targeted rainbow table. Usage: sample_or_example_code only — not production.

revised_context_bundles.reviewer_note: Revised (FU-001): IV generation method body (lines 287-317) and encrypt() prepend logic (lines 319-348) now fully captured. SecureRandom.nextBytes() IV generation confirmed. DEFAULT_KEY_SIZE=128, initializationVectorSize=128, SHA1PRNG algorithm confirmed. Mandatory full method body rule now satisfied.

Revised (FU-002): All default parameters now confirmed from source: DEFAULT_ITERATIONS=1, DEFAULT_MEMORY_KIB=65536 (64 MiB), DEFAULT_PARALLELISM=4, DEFAULT_OUTPUT_LENGTH_BITS=256, SALT_LENGTH_BITS=128. OWASP note: t=1 is at minimum boundary — memory=64 MiB compensates. Full generate() call chain confirmed.

known_limitations: Repository-wide caller search for BlowfishCipherService instantiation returned no production callers in grep hits. Only presence in public API surface confirmed.
revised_context_bundles.reviewer_note: Revised (FU-003): Full BlowfishCipherService class body confirmed. ALGORITHM_NAME='Blowfish', BLOCK_SIZE=64, default mode CBC, PKCS5 padding, IV size 64 bits. No production caller found — class is available via public API but appears to be legacy dead code. 64-bit block size confirms weak_algorithm security concern.

Revised (FU-004): Full PaddingScheme enum body confirmed. 11 values: NONE, ISO10126, OAEP, OAEPWithMd5AndMgf1, OAEPWithSha1AndMgf1, OAEPWithSha256AndMgf1, OAEPWithSha384AndMgf1, OAEPWithSha512AndMgf1, PKCS1, PKCS5, SSL3. Security concerns: PKCS1Padding (Bleichenbacher-vulnerable for RSA), OAEPWithMd5AndMgf1 (MD5 in OAEP), SSL3Padding (deprecated SSL context).

known_limitations: Hash algorithm not determined within JdbcRealm itself — delegated to externally configured CredentialsMatcher. No CredentialsMatcher configuration file available in this context bundle.
revised_context_bundles.reviewer_note: Revised (FU-005): Full doGetAuthenticationInfo() body confirmed. Default saltStyle=NO_SALT — returns raw DB password to CredentialsMatcher. Hash algorithm delegated to CredentialsMatcher (external). getSaltForUser() returns username as salt (insecure default). EXTERNAL case insecure salt confirmed from source. No plaintext comparison occurs in JdbcRealm — comparison delegated to matcher.

known_limitations: Hash algorithm not specified in SaltAwareJdbcRealm — delegated to CredentialsMatcher configured in Spring XML.
revised_context_bundles.reviewer_note: Revised (FU-006): Full SaltAwareJdbcRealm body confirmed. Sample code under samples/spring-mvc. Salt = username (ByteSource.Util.bytes(username)) — explicitly warned as 'very bad example / DON'T DO THIS IN A REAL APP' in source comment. This is an insecure sample demonstrating what NOT to do. usage_context: sample_or_example_code.
